backlog.title: Status-line layout & fuzzy ranking (PRD §19 + §20)
backlog.description: The central rework: turn the picker from a substring filter with a trailing index/total into a fuzzy-ranked, scrollable, query-bar status line. §19 (layout: query bar pinned far-left + ranked tabs in a left-to-right scroll viewport + overflow indicators, count removed) and §20 (ranking: subsequence match + score) are one phase because the layout renders the order the ranker produces, and both feed the same shared lp_rank the renderer and input-handler index into. Replaces scripts/filter.sh with scripts/rank.sh; adds scripts/layout.sh; reworks renderer.sh; wires scroll state into input paths.

milestones.title: Foundation: rank.sh, layout.sh, options, state keys
milestones.description: Add the two new sourced libs (rank.sh superseding filter.sh; layout.sh viewport math), the new options, and the new runtime state keys. No user-visible behavior change yet (the libs are unused until M2/M3); this lands the integration seams the later milestones plug into.

tasks.title: rank.sh fuzzy ranker (supersedes filter.sh)
tasks.description: Create scripts/rank.sh defining lp_rank LIST FILTER that prints matching session names best-first (drop-in for lp_build_filtered's callers), then switch the 6 call sites from filter.sh to rank.sh and retire filter.sh. Match rule: subsequence (chars in order, case-insensitive); non-matches hidden; empty filter = all names at score 0 in original tmux order (no reordering). Score: prefix > word-boundary > contiguous > early-position; stable tie-break.

subtasks.title: Implement lp_rank subsequence match + scoring algorithm
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_77ef311abf10/architecture/codebase_patterns.md §P1 (sourced lib contract: NO source-time side effects, set -u, no -e). lp_rank is pure bash, O(N·Q), no tmux calls — it must stay under the §18 renderer budget (see empirical_findings.md). The function it replaces is scripts/filter.sh::lp_build_filtered (read it for the exact call convention).
2. INPUT: $1 = LIST (newline-separated session names, as stored in @livepicker-list); $2 = FILTER (the raw query string, may be empty). Both passed as positional args.
3. LOGIC: Implement in a NEW file scripts/rank.sh. Match rule (PRD §20 §3.36): a name matches iff every FILTER char appears in the name IN ORDER, case-insensitively (subsequence, not contiguous). Non-matches HIDDEN entirely (so create-on-empty still fires). Scoring (§20 §3.37, accumulate over matched positions, higher=better): large PREFIX bonus (first query char at name pos 0) > WORD-BOUNDARY bonus (pos 0 or after a non-alphanumeric -_. / or camelCase boundary) > CONTIGUITY bonus (matched char immediately follows the previous matched char) > small POSITION penalty (proportional to match start offset). Stable tie-break on ORIGINAL tmux order (do NOT reorder equal scores). Empty FILTER (§20 §3.38): print ALL names at score 0 in original order (byte-identical to lp_build_filtered's empty-filter output — CRITICAL so existing tests stay green). Source-once-per-process, tight loop, NO per-name subshell (no `$(...)` inside the name loop). Lowercase both sides via `${var,,}` (bash 4+; the shebang is #!/usr/bin/env bash).
4. OUTPUT: Prints matching names best-first, one per line, preserving original CASE. Caller does: mapfile -t ranked < <(lp_rank "$LIST" "$FILTER"). Empty LIST prints nothing; empty FILTER prints all (original order).
5. DOCS: none — rank.sh is an internal sourced lib (no user-facing/config/API surface). Mode A: no doc update needed here.

subtasks.title: Switch 6 filter.sh call sites to rank.sh; retire filter.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: filter.sh::lp_build_filtered is sourced by renderer.sh (TWO call sites: the §17 window-status early-return path AND the plain path) and input-handler.sh (FOUR call sites: _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match, next-session, prev-session, confirm). grep -n 'lp_build_filtered\|filter.sh' scripts/ to find all 6. The switch must be BYTE-IDENTICAL for an empty filter (rank.sh S1 guarantees original-order output on empty filter) so the 44-green suite stays green.
2. INPUT: lp_rank (from P1.M1.T1.S1) with the identical call convention `lp_rank "$LIST" "$FILTER"`.
3. LOGIC: In each of the 6 call sites, replace `lp_build_filtered` with `lp_rank` (the call shape is identical: `mapfile -t filtered < <(lp_rank "$LIST" "$FILTER")`). Update each consumer's `# shellcheck source=filter.sh` directive to `# shellcheck source=rank.sh` and change the `source "$CURRENT_DIR/filter.sh"` line to `source "$CURRENT_DIR/rank.sh"`. Then DELETE scripts/filter.sh (fully superseded — do not leave divergent substring logic). Verify shellcheck -x scripts/*.sh passes and tests/run.sh is green.
4. OUTPUT: filter.sh removed; renderer.sh + input-handler.sh source rank.sh and call lp_rank. No behavior change for empty/common filters (substring⊆subsequence; subsequence is a superset match set, but scoring only reorders when ≥1 char typed — empty query unchanged).
5. DOCS: none — internal refactor.

tasks.title: layout.sh viewport math + tab-width measurement
tasks.description: Create scripts/layout.sh defining the display-width measurement + scroll helpers, sourced by BOTH renderer.sh (to slice the visible window) and input-handler.sh (to scroll-into-view), so they can never disagree (PRD §16 'Viewport measurement'). Pure functions: read args, return values; no tmux calls (width comes from the cached @livepicker-client-width).

subtasks.title: Implement display-width measurement + lp_viewport scroll math
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §16 'Viewport measurement' — tab display width must be measured with #[…] style directives STRIPPED FIRST (they are zero-width but inflate the raw string length). The measurement lives ONCE here and is used by both the renderer (slice visible window) and input-handler (scroll-into-view) so they cannot disagree. Width comes from the cached @livepicker-client-width option (captured at activate, P1.M3.T1), NOT a tmux round-trip (§18 budget). See codebase_patterns.md §P7.
2. INPUT: A NEW sourced lib scripts/layout.sh with PURE bash functions (no tmux calls, no source-time side effects). Inputs are passed as args/state reads: the ranked tab strings, the cached client width, the query block (icon+query+gap), and the highlight index.
3. LOGIC: Implement (a) a display-width function lp_disp_width STRING that strips every `#[…]` run (regex strip of `#\[[^]]*\]`) then counts visible columns (the nerd-font icon U+F002 is a single narrow codepoint = width 1; ASCII chars = 1; use a byte/column counter — wc -m after stripping, or a manual loop). (b) lp_viewport: given the array of ranked tabs, available tab width T = client_width − query_block_width − active_indicators_width, the current scroll index, and the highlight index, compute and PRINT/RETURN: the first-visible scroll index (clamped so the highlight is visible), the visible slice [start,end], and the hidden-left + hidden-right counts (their SUM is the %d for the +N> indicator). Scroll-into-view rule (PRD §19 §3.32): if index < scroll, scroll=index; if the highlight tab's right edge exceeds scroll+T, advance scroll until it fits; every call clamps scroll=0 when the whole list fits in T. Keep these as pure functions the renderer/input-handler call with their own state reads.
4. OUTPUT: scripts/layout.sh exports lp_disp_width + lp_viewport (+ internal helpers). Renderer and input-handler will source it (in M2/M3) and pass cached client_width; this subtask ONLY creates the lib (not yet wired in). No tmux mutation.
5. DOCS: none — internal lib.

tasks.title: New options + state keys (integration seams)
tasks.description: Add the new @livepicker-* option accessors and runtime/saved-state keys as inert seams. Add: opt_nerd_fonts, opt_search_icon, opt_query_gap, opt_overflow_left, opt_overflow_right_format. Add STATE_SCROLL, STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH, ORIG_CLIENT_RESIZED_HOOK. Do NOT remove opt_show_count here (it stays referenced by renderer until M2.T3 removes its last consumer atomically).

subtasks.title: Add P1 layout option accessors to options.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: options.sh uses the one-liner `opt_<name>() { get_opt "@livepicker-<suffix>" "<default>"; }` pattern (read scripts/options.sh for exact style). Defaults are PRD §11 verbatim.
2. INPUT: The existing get_opt helper in options.sh.
3. LOGIC: Add these accessors after opt_preview_defer, each a one-liner: opt_nerd_fonts() default "on"; opt_search_icon() default "\uf002" (emit the raw UTF-8 byte sequence U+F002 — nf-fa-search; the literal in the source is the 3-byte UTF-8); opt_query_gap() default "2"; opt_overflow_left() default "<"; opt_overflow_right_format() default "+%d>". Do NOT remove opt_show_count (deferred to P1.M2.T3 where its last consumer is removed atomically).
4. OUTPUT: options.sh defines the 5 new accessors; existing accessors untouched. Each returns the user-set value or the PRD default.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add the 5 new rows to README.md's Configuration table (@livepicker-nerd-fonts, @livepicker-search-icon, @livepicker-query-gap, @livepicker-overflow-left, @livepicker-overflow-right-format) with their defaults. This rides WITH the work (per SOW §5 Mode A). Full prose/overview lands in P4.

subtasks.title: Add STATE_SCROLL, STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH, ORIG_CLIENT_RESIZED_HOOK + clear-list
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: state.sh's _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS is the MANDATORY clear-list — clear_all_state unsets exactly those keys (see codebase_patterns.md §P3 and state.sh comments 'else they leak across picker sessions'). New ORIG_* saved keys are auto-caught by clear_all_state's `grep '@livepicker-orig-'`. The client-resized hook save/restore mirrors session-window-changed exactly (empirical_findings.md Finding 4; codebase_patterns.md §P4).
2. INPUT: The existing readonly-constant + _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS pattern in state.sh.
3. LOGIC: Add `readonly STATE_SCROLL="@livepicker-scroll"`, `readonly STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH="@livepicker-client-width"` to the runtime STATE_* block; APPEND both to the _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS space-list (MANDATORY). Add `readonly ORIG_CLIENT_RESIZED_HOOK="@livepicker-orig-client-resized"` to the saved-state ORIG_* block (it is auto-cleared by the @livepicker-orig- grep). Do NOT initialize values here (activation does that in P1.M3.T1).
4. OUTPUT: state.sh defines the 3 new constants; clear_all_state will tear down STATE_SCROLL + STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH via the updated clear-list. No behavior change yet.
5. DOCS: none — internal state keys.

milestones.title: Renderer rework (query bar + ranked viewport + overflow; drop count)
milestones.description: Restructure renderer.sh render() so line 1 follows §19 for BOTH tab styles (plain and window-status). Both paths go through: query block (icon+query, only while typing) → ranked tabs sliced by @livepicker-scroll → overflow indicators. Drop the index/total count and the query> label entirely. Factor the query-bar + viewport assembly via layout.sh so the two tab-styles share one layout engine (the only difference is how a single tab renders).

tasks.title: Plain render path §19 restructure (query-empty / query-active / no-match)
tasks.description: Restructure the plain (non-window-status) render path in render() to follow §19: query-empty shows only tabs justified per status-justify (no icon/query/gap/count); query-active shows icon+query+gap+tabs at column 0; no-match shows icon+query+(no match). Drop the SHOW_COUNT branch from the plain path. Use lp_rank (ranked order) and lp_disp_width (from layout.sh).

subtasks.title: Restructure render() plain path into §19 layout; drop plain SHOW_COUNT branch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: renderer.sh render() has TWO paths: the §17 window-status early-return (leave UNTOUCHED in this subtask — it is reworked in P1.M2.T3) and the plain path (the `LIST=...; ... mapfile -t filtered < <(lp_rank ...) ...` block BELOW the ws early-return). Read scripts/renderer.sh for the load-bearing rules (codebase_patterns.md §P6): emit EXACTLY ONE line no trailing newline (`printf '%s' $out`); #[default] after every segment; process-substitution list read; render || fallback-red; NO set -e; PURE+FAST (option reads + pure-bash measure only; width from cached @livepicker-client-width via get_state, NEVER display-message). The query text MUST double every # (`${FILTER//\#/##}`) so tmux renders it literally (#() output is not re-parsed for #{…}).
2. INPUT: lp_rank (P1.M1.T1) for ranked order; lp_disp_width (P1.M1.T2) for measurement; opt_search_icon/opt_nerd_fonts/opt_query_gap (P1.M1.T3.S1); STATE_LIST/STATE_FILTER/STATE_INDEX/STATE_SCROLL/STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH via get_state. Source layout.sh.
3. LOGIC: Branch on FILTER emptiness. (a) Query EMPTY (§19 §3.30): line shows ONLY the session tabs (lp_rank with empty filter = original order), positioned per status-justify (read `tmux show-options -g -v status-justify` ONCE — but NOTE this is a tmux read; it is acceptable because it is a single read per redraw, not per-tab; cache concern is per-tab round-trips). No icon, no query, no gap, no count. Highlight STATE_INDEX with HFG/HBG. Tabs joined by a single space (plain mode). (b) Query ACTIVE ≥1 char (§19 §3.31): `column 0: <icon><query><gap><tabs...>`. icon = opt_search_icon if opt_nerd_fonts on else empty (emit raw UTF-8 bytes); query = STATE_FILTER with # doubled; gap = exactly opt_query_gap spaces; status-justify SUSPENDED for tabs while query active (pinned-left flow). Highlight STATE_INDEX. (c) No-match (§19 §3.34, lp_rank returns 0 names): `<icon><query> (no match)` in plain ASCII (shown regardless of nerd-font mode). No tabs, no indicators. In ALL three: remove the `[ "$SHOW_COUNT" -eq 1 ]` suffix from the PLAIN path only (keep SHOW_COUNT_RAW/SHOW_COUNT locals + opt_show_count for now — the ws path in P1.M2.T3 still uses them; removing opt_show_count here would break the ws path). Use STATE_SCROLL to slice the visible tabs (full windowing/overflow lands in P1.M2.T2; here just read scroll and show the full ranked list — overflow indicators come next).
4. OUTPUT: render() plain path follows §19 with no count. The ws path still works (unchanged). All existing renderer tests that asserted the old `query> FILTER [i/N]` plain output will need updating (the count is gone) — update them as the TDD step.
5. DOCS: none beyond P1.M1.T3.S1's table rows — renderer output is internal status content.

tasks.title: Viewport windowing + overflow indicators (plain path)
tasks.description: Window the ranked list by @livepicker-scroll to width T (via layout.sh lp_viewport) and render the overflow indicators: right +N> (%d = TOTAL hidden, left+right combined) and left < (presence only, when scroll>0). Both can show at once (< …tabs… +N>); neither when all fit. Apply to the plain query-active and query-empty paths.

subtasks.title: Window ranked list by scroll + render overflow indicators
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §19 §3.33 — right indicator opt_overflow_right_format (default +%d>) where %d is the TOTAL hidden tabs (left-hidden + right-hidden COMBINED, not split); left indicator opt_overflow_left (default <), presence only when scroll>0. Both can show at once (< …visible tabs… +N>); neither when everything fits. Indicators are CHROME (styled @livepicker-fg/bg), never highlighted, never counted as tabs. Available tab width T = client_width − query_block_width − active_indicators_width (recompute every redraw; query length changes). Use layout.sh lp_viewport (P1.M1.T2) for the slice + hidden counts. Clamp scroll=0 when the list fits.
2. INPUT: lp_viewport (P1.M1.T2.S1) returning the visible slice + hidden-left/hidden-right counts; STATE_SCROLL + STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH; opt_overflow_left/opt_overflow_right_format; the plain-path render structure from P1.M2.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: In the plain render path (both query-empty-overflow and query-active), after building the ranked tabs, call lp_viewport to slice to [scroll, scroll+T) and get hidden counts. Render: if scroll>0, prepend opt_overflow_left styled with FG/BG immediately after the gap (before the first visible tab). If total_hidden>0, append opt_overflow_right_format with %d→total_hidden at the far right of the tab region. Substitute %d in the format string (printf or parameter expansion). The query-empty case: if tabs overflow, justification is moot (every column occupied) so tabs flow left-to-right from column 0 with the indicators. When all fit, neither indicator shows and (query-empty) status-justify positions them.
4. OUTPUT: render() plain path shows a scrollable viewport with correct overflow indicators in both query states.
5. DOCS: none — internal renderer.

tasks.title: Window-status (§17) path §19 restructure + remove SHOW_COUNT entirely
tasks.description: Rework the §17 window-status early-return path so it ALSO goes through the §19 layout (query bar + viewport + overflow) instead of bypassing it. Then remove opt_show_count, the SHOW_COUNT_RAW/SHOW_COUNT locals, and every remaining SHOW_COUNT branch (now only the ws path had them) — the index/total count is gone everywhere. Both tab-styles share the §19 layout engine; the only per-style difference is how one tab renders (cached template swap vs #[fg/bg]name).

subtasks.title: Rework window-status path through §19 viewport; remove opt_show_count + all SHOW_COUNT logic
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The §17 ws path is the `if [ "$(opt_tab_style)" = "window-status" ]` early-return block in render(). It currently joins ALL ranked tabs with the cached templates + window-status-separator and appends a SHOW_COUNT suffix — it BYPASSES §19. PRD §17 diff (confirmed in delta_prd): 'status-justify positions the tabs only when there is no query and the tabs fit; otherwise the section 19 viewport rules apply.' So the ws path must ALSO: show the query bar (icon+query+gap) when typing, slice by scroll, and show overflow indicators. The cached templates (STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL/STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL with __lp_tab__/__lp_sentinel__ placeholders swapped per §17) are how ONE tab renders; the layout (query bar/viewport/overflow) is shared. Factor the query-bar+viewport+indicator assembly (from P1.M2.T1/P1.M2.T2) so both styles plug into ONE engine — do NOT duplicate layout logic.
2. INPUT: The plain-path §19 layout engine (P1.M2.T1.S1 + P1.M2.T2.S1); the cached ws templates + window-status-separator read; lp_viewport.
3. LOGIC: Restructure render() so there is ONE §19 layout flow, parameterized by a 'render one tab' function: plain uses `#[fg=HFG,bg=HBG]name#[default]`; window-status swaps __lp_tab__/__lp_sentinel__→name in the cached template (current vs inactive per index). Both apply: query-empty (tabs justified, no chrome), query-active (icon+query+gap+tabs), no-match, viewport windowing by scroll, overflow indicators, tabs joined by window-status-separator (ws) or single space (plain). THEN remove opt_show_count from options.sh, delete the SHOW_COUNT_RAW/SHOW_COUNT locals and EVERY remaining SHOW_COUNT branch (now gone from both paths). If either ws template is empty, fall back to plain (existing behavior). Verify the §17 fallback (empty/unresolvable template → plain) still triggers.
4. OUTPUT: render() has one §19-compliant layout engine for both styles; the index/total count and query> label exist NOWHERE. opt_show_count is deleted.
5. DOCS: none — internal renderer. (P1.M1.T3.S1 already added the table rows; this removes the show-count row implicitly — note in P4 that show-count is removed.)

milestones.title: Input flow: scroll-into-view, scroll reset, client-width cache
milestones.description: Wire the scroll state into the input paths and add the client-width capture + client-resized hook to activate/restore. The client-width cache is what the renderer measures the viewport against (no per-keystroke tmux round-trip).

tasks.title: Client-width capture + client-resized hook (activate + restore)
tasks.description: Capture the invoking client's width into @livepicker-client-width at activation, install a client-resized hook that refreshes it for the picker duration, and restore the prior hook exactly on exit. Mirror the existing session-window-changed save/restore.

subtasks.title: Capture client_width at activate + install/restore client-resized hook
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: empirical_findings.md Finding 4 — `display-message -p '#{client_width}'` against the invoking client returns the width; use the existing lp_client_format helper (utils.sh) to resolve client-awarely (client-aware; falls back to context-free on detached/test edge). The client-resized hook save/restore is IDENTICAL in shape to session-window-changed (codebase_patterns.md §P4): tmux_get_hook returns full multi-line show-hooks output; tmux_clear_hook clears every index; restore parses name[N] <cmd> lines and replays via set-hook -g name[$N] preserving index + -b. empirical Finding 4 confirmed client-resized installs as client-resized[0] and shows in show-hooks -g.
2. INPUT: STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH + ORIG_CLIENT_RESIZED_HOOK (P1.M1.T3.S2); lp_client_format (utils.sh); tmux_get_hook/tmux_clear_hook (utils.sh).
3. LOGIC: livepicker.sh activate_main — after building the list (STEP T2) and BEFORE the status grow: capture `set_state "$STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH" "$(lp_client_format '#{client_width}')"`; save the prior client-resized hook `tmux set-option -g "$ORIG_CLIENT_RESIZED_HOOK" "$(tmux_get_hook client-resized)"`; install `tmux set-hook -g client-resized "run-shell '<abs>/input-handler.sh refresh-width'"` (or an inline refresh that re-reads client_width into STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH). restore.sh STEP 4: mirror the session-window-changed restore — replay every saved client-resized[N] <cmd> line preserving index + -b; if nothing saved, leave it cleared. Add a tiny refresh-width action (or inline) to input-handler.sh that re-captures client_width via lp_client_format and refreshes status. Update the §9 save/restore doc-comments.
4. OUTPUT: activate captures + hooks client_width; restore puts back the exact prior client-resized hook. The renderer reads STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH (no per-keystroke round-trip).
5. DOCS: none — internal activate/restore. Update the §9/§10 in-code comments to match.

tasks.title: Scroll-into-view on nav + scroll reset on type/backspace/cancel-clear
tasks.description: Wire the scroll state into the input paths: next-session/prev-session run the layout.sh scroll-into-view rule after updating the index; type/backspace/cancel-clear reset scroll=0 alongside the existing index=0 reset. Scroll is part of the synchronous status update (still status-only; no preview work).

subtasks.title: Wire scroll state into input paths (scroll-into-view + reset)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: input-handler.sh input_main() dispatches type/backspace/next-session/prev-session/confirm/cancel. It already uses _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match (type/backspace/cancel-clear) and _lp_preview_follow (next/prev) for the deferred preview (codebase_patterns.md §P5 — do NOT bypass; route new preview re-syncs through it). It uses lp_rank (after P1.M1.T1.S2). Scroll is a STATE write only — it is part of the synchronous status update (refresh-client -S redraws the renderer which reads STATE_SCROLL). NO preview work is added on the type/backspace path (§18 contract). See layout.sh lp_viewport for the scroll-into-view math (P1.M1.T2).
2. INPUT: lp_viewport (P1.M1.T2.S1) scroll-into-view rule; STATE_SCROLL (P1.M1.T3.S2); lp_rank for the ranked list; STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH for T.
3. LOGIC: next-session/prev-session — AFTER set_state INDEX and BEFORE _lp_preview_follow: compute the ranked list (lp_rank), read STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH, run lp_viewport's scroll-into-view rule (if index<scroll set scroll=index; if the highlight tab's right edge exceeds scroll+T advance scroll until it fits) and set_state STATE_SCROLL to the result. type/backspace/the cancel-clear branch — add `set_state "$STATE_SCROLL" "0"` alongside the existing `set_state "$STATE_INDEX" "0"` reset. Update the comments that claim type/backspace are 'status-only' to note scroll reset is part of the synchronous status update (still status-only — scroll is just a state write, no preview work).
4. OUTPUT: nav scrolls the highlight into view; type/backspace/cancel-clear reset scroll to 0. The highlight tracks the keypress synchronously; the preview re-sync stays deferred.
5. DOCS: none — internal input handling.

milestones.title: Validation (§15.28 layout / ranking / scroll)
milestones.description: New/updated integration test suites under the existing socket-isolated harness, mirroring the new §15.28 checklist. Renderer tests need no client (seed state → run renderer.sh → assert_contains); nav/scroll tests need attach_test_client.

tasks.title: test_layout.sh — renderer layout integration suite
tasks.description: Assert the §19 layout: empty query → only tabs justified, no icon/query/count; one char → icon+query at column 0, exactly query-gap spaces, ranked tabs; overflow → +N> with N=total hidden, < after scrolling, neither when all fit; no-match → (no match); no index/total anywhere. Assert the window-status path also honors §19.

subtasks.title: Create test_layout.sh (empty/active/overflow/no-match/no-count, both tab styles)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: tests/test_functional.sh + tests/test_appearance.sh use the renderer-seed idiom (no client needed): set @livepicker-* state via tmux set-option -g, run scripts/renderer.sh, assert_contains on stdout. tests/helpers.sh provides setup_test (isolated -L socket, pins @livepicker-preview-defer off), assert_eq/assert_contains/fail. See codebase_patterns.md §P8. The renderer reads STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH — seed it for viewport tests. For status-justify assertion, set status-justify on the test socket.
2. INPUT: The §19-compliant renderer (P1.M2.T3.S1); the test harness.
3. LOGIC: Create tests/test_layout.sh. Tests (each setup_test + seed + run renderer.sh + assert): (a) empty query → output contains only tab names, NO icon glyph, NO query text, NO '[', NO 'query>', NO '/'. (b) one char query 'l' → output starts with the search icon (or query text if nerd-fonts off) then query 'l' then exactly opt_query_gap spaces then ranked tabs; top match highlighted (HFG/HBG). (c) overflow: seed a client_width too small for many tabs → output contains '+N>' where N = total hidden; after simulating scroll>0 (set STATE_SCROLL) output contains '<'; when tabs fit, neither indicator. (d) no-match → output contains '(no match)'. (e) assert NO 'index/total' pattern (no '[0/5]' anywhere) in ANY state. (f) window-status tab-style path: seed STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL/STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL + tab-style window-status → assert it STILL shows the query bar + viewport + overflow (not the old full-line join) and honors §19.
4. OUTPUT: tests/test_layout.sh passes under tests/run.sh; covers §15.28 layout items.
5. DOCS: none.

tasks.title: test_ranking.sh — fuzzy ranking integration suite
tasks.description: Assert the §20 ranking: query 'log' → logs-prod outranks blog-engine (prefix > deep subsequence); a non-subsequence name is hidden; empty query preserves tmux order (no reordering); renderer/nav/confirm all agree on ranked[index] (no drift). Assert performance is within the §18 budget for N up to a few hundred.

subtasks.title: Create test_ranking.sh (prefix>subsequence, hidden, empty order, no-drift, perf)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: rank.sh::lp_rank is a pure function — test it directly by sourcing scripts/rank.sh and calling lp_rank (no tmux needed), OR via the renderer-seed idiom. The no-drift contract: renderer, next-session, prev-session, confirm all call lp_rank with the same LIST+FILTER, so ranked[index] is identical across them (single source of truth). Performance: O(N·Q) in pure bash; for N=300, Q=4 it must complete well under 50ms (assert via a bounded timing check or a cap on subshell spawns — grep the source to confirm no `$(...)` inside the name loop).
2. INPUT: lp_rank (P1.M1.T1).
3. LOGIC: Create tests/test_ranking.sh. Tests: (a) source rank.sh; lp_rank with LIST='blog-engine\nlogs-prod\nalog' FILTER='log' → first line is 'logs-prod' (prefix bonus beats blog-engine's deep subsequence). (b) a name that is NOT a subsequence (e.g. 'xyz' vs filter 'log') is absent from output. (c) empty FILTER → output equals the input LIST in original order (diff). (d) no-drift: seed state, run renderer.sh and capture the tab order; separately simulate next-session and confirm and assert they resolve the same ranked[index] (the highlight). (e) perf: build a 300-name list, time `lp_rank`, assert < 50ms (allow a generous bound; the point is no per-name subshell).
4. OUTPUT: tests/test_ranking.sh passes; covers §15.28 ranking items.
5. DOCS: none.

tasks.title: Scroll-into-view + client-width cache tests
tasks.description: Assert: nav past the right edge scrolls the highlight into view and shows <; resizing the client updates @livepicker-client-width via the client-resized hook and the next redraw recomputes the viewport; after exit the prior client-resized hook is restored exactly; type/backspace/cancel-clear reset scroll to 0.

subtasks.title: Create scroll + client-width cache tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: scroll/nav tests need attach_test_client (the input handler runs refresh-client -S which needs a client; and client_width capture needs a client). Mirror tests/test_functional.sh's attach_test_client usage. The client-resized hook can be fired on the test socket by resizing the client window or by directly invoking the hook's refresh. codebase_patterns.md §P8.
2. INPUT: The scroll-wired input paths (P1.M3.T2) + client-width cache (P1.M3.T1); the test harness with attach_test_client.
3. LOGIC: Create tests/test_scroll_width.sh (or extend an existing test). Tests: (a) seed many sessions + small client_width; activate picker; send next-session repeatedly past the right edge → assert STATE_SCROLL advanced and renderer output shows '<'. (b) resize the client (or re-fire client-resized) → assert @livepicker-client-width updated and next redraw recomputes the viewport. (c) exit picker → assert the prior client-resized hook is restored exactly (diff show-hooks -g client-resized before vs after). (d) type/backspace/cancel-clear → assert STATE_SCROLL resets to 0. Use the isolated -L socket; teardown kills it.
4. OUTPUT: tests pass; covers §15.28 scroll + width-cache items.
5. DOCS: none.

backlog.title: Session management: rename / delete (PRD §21)
backlog.description: Depends on P1: rename/delete resolve the highlighted item via lp_rank + @livepicker-index, and re-rank after the mutation. Adds scripts/session-mgmt.sh, three options (rename-key, delete-key, confirm-delete), two key bindings (activate step 5), and two input-handler actions (rename, delete). Session-CRUD parity with tmux-sessionx so livepicker can replace it.

milestones.title: session-mgmt.sh + bindings + input dispatch + options
milestones.description: Add the rename/delete options and activate binding step 5 (both control keys, non-alphanumeric, so they never collide with the typing set), then implement scripts/session-mgmt.sh (rename via command-prompt + do-rename; delete with guards + unlink-first + kill-session + re-sync) and the input-handler rename/delete dispatch.

tasks.title: Options + key bindings (activate step 5)
tasks.description: Add opt_rename_key (C-r), opt_delete_key (M-BSpace), opt_confirm_delete (off) to options.sh. In livepicker.sh activate T4, after the nav bindings (step 4), add step 5: bind rename-key → input-handler.sh rename, delete-key → input-handler.sh delete. Both are control keys (distinct tmux keys from the letters r/backspace), so they override any copied same-key binding without colliding with the typing set.

subtasks.title: Add rename/delete/confirm-delete options + activate binding step 5
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: options.sh one-liner pattern (codebase_patterns.md §P2). livepicker.sh activate T4 binds keys in order (copy → typing → backspace/confirm/cancel → nav); the load-bearing rule (PRD §8 §3.18): tmux keeps the LAST binding for a key in a table, and steps 4/5 run AFTER step 2 (typing), so a nav/mgmt key OVERRIDES a typing binding for the same key — which is why mgmt keys must be non-alphanumeric (C-r and M-BSpace are distinct tmux keys from the letters r and BSpace). The binding is INERT until P2.M1.T2 implements the dispatch (bind-key stores the command string; tmux only runs it on keypress). Mirror the existing nav-binding block (the `for lp_c in ...` loops).
2. INPUT: get_opt (options.sh); the activate T4 binding structure in livepicker.sh.
3. LOGIC: options.sh — add opt_rename_key() default "C-r", opt_delete_key() default "M-BSpace", opt_confirm_delete() default "off". livepicker.sh activate T4 — AFTER the nav `for lp_c in $(opt_prev_key)...` block, add: `tmux bind-key -T livepicker "$(opt_rename_key)" run-shell "$CURRENT_DIR/input-handler.sh rename"` and `tmux bind-key -T livepicker "$(opt_delete_key)" run-shell "$CURRENT_DIR/input-handler.sh delete"`. Single-key accessors (not space-list) per PRD §11.
4. OUTPUT: the 2 keys are bound in the livepicker table; the 3 options exist. Inert until dispatch lands in P2.M1.T2.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add @livepicker-rename-key, @livepicker-delete-key, @livepicker-confirm-delete rows to README.md Configuration table; add the rename/delete keybinds + 'driver/last-session refuse' + 'delete key terminal caveat (M-BSpace may be stripped on some terminals; rebind C-h/DC)' notes to README.md Usage. Full prose in P4.

tasks.title: session-mgmt.sh rename + delete + input dispatch
tasks.description: Add `rename` and `delete` actions to input-handler.sh input_main() that delegate to scripts/session-mgmt.sh. Implement rename (command-prompt pre-filled + do-rename with sanitized/collision guards) and delete (driver/last-session guards + optional confirm + unlink-first-then-kill-session + re-rank + re-sync). Both resolve the highlighted item via lp_rank + @livepicker-index exactly as confirm/nav do.

subtasks.title: Implement rename (command-prompt + do-rename) + input dispatch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §21 §3.42 + §13. rename uses `tmux command-prompt -I "$S" -p "Rename session:" "run-shell '$SCRIPT_DIR/session-mgmt.sh do-rename %%'"`. While command-prompt is open the livepicker table is SUSPENDED (the prompt captures input); on submit/escape tmux restores the livepicker table — so no extra binding work. Sanitization (§16): tmux sanitizes session names (`:`→`_`, leading `.`→`_`) and a rename to an EXISTING name errors. Detect a sanitized result (the resulting exact session name ≠ $NEW) or a collision and display-message + abort rather than silently mis-renaming. Escaping caveat: `%%` inside the single-quoted run-shell handles normal names; names with `'`/`"`/`` ` ``/`$` may break — known limitation (document in P4). A rename does NOT change the window id, so NO preview re-link is needed.
2. INPUT: lp_rank (P1.M1.T1) + STATE_INDEX to resolve the highlighted session S from ranked[index]; STATE_LIST to rewrite; ORIG_* for guards; the binding from P2.M1.T1.S1.
3. LOGIC: Create scripts/session-mgmt.sh (executable entry, sources options/utils/state/rank, has a session_mgmt_main driver). input-handler.sh: add `rename)` action → delegates to `session-mgmt.sh rename`. session-mgmt.sh rename: resolve S from lp_rank+STATE_INDEX (no-op if ranked empty); open `command-prompt -I "$S" -p "Rename session:" "run-shell '<abs>/session-mgmt.sh do-rename %%"`. do-rename NEW: re-resolve S from the current index (stable during the prompt); empty NEW → no-op; `rename-session -t "=$S" "$NEW"`; if the resulting exact name (capture via `tmux display-message -p -t "=$NEW" '#{session_name}'` or list-sessions) ≠ $NEW, or rename-session rc≠0 (collision), display-message + abort; on success rewrite STATE_LIST (replace old name with the actual resulting name), keep the highlight on it, refresh-client -S (no preview re-link). Window mode: rename-window on the highlighted window, same shape.
4. OUTPUT: C-r opens the prompt pre-filled; a valid new name updates the tab in place and keeps the highlight; sanitized/collision names abort with a message and no rename; picker stays open.
5. DOCS: none beyond P2.M1.T1.S1 (the escaping caveat + caveats ride there / P4).

subtasks.title: Implement delete (guards + unlink-first + kill-session + re-sync) + input dispatch
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: empirical_findings.md Finding 3 (CONFIRMED) — kill-session does NOT remove a linked window from the driver; the preview window SURVIVES as an orphan. So do-delete MUST unlink-window the linked preview from the driver FIRST (when STATE_LINKED_ID belongs to the victim) before kill-session. Use unlink-window WITHOUT -k (removes one link; the kill-session then destroys the window in its now-only session). Guards (PRD §21 §3.43): refuse (display-message, no kill) if S==ORIG_SESSION (killing the driver detaches the client) or if STATE_LIST has length 1 (tmux requires ≥1 session). confirm-before (PRD §13) is optional (opt_confirm_delete). After kill: rewrite STATE_LIST (drop S), clamp index to min(index, new_len-1), re-rank, re-sync the preview to the new highlight (route through the existing _lp_preview_follow / preview.sh — the unlink+kill cleared the old preview; this links the new highlight's window).
2. INPUT: lp_rank + STATE_INDEX to resolve S; STATE_LINKED_ID + ORIG_SESSION for the unlink guard; opt_confirm_delete; the preview entry point (_lp_preview_follow).
3. LOGIC: session-mgmt.sh: add `delete)` and `do-delete)` actions. input-handler.sh: add `delete)` action → delegates to `session-mgmt.sh delete`. delete: resolve S (no-op if ranked empty); guards (S==ORIG_SESSION → display-message 'cannot delete the driver session' + return; list length 1 → display-message 'cannot delete the last session' + return); if opt_confirm_delete on → `confirm-before -p "Kill session $S? (y/n)" "run-shell '<abs>/session-mgmt.sh do-delete $S"` and return; else call do-delete directly. do-delete S: if STATE_LINKED_ID is set AND belongs to S (i.e. the currently-previewed window is S's), `unlink-window -t "$ORIG_SESSION:$linked_id" 2>/dev/null || true` FIRST (prevents the orphan leak); `kill-session -t "=$S" 2>/dev/null`; rewrite STATE_LIST (drop S); clamp STATE_INDEX to min(index, new_len-1); recompute the ranked view (lp_rank); re-sync the preview to the new highlight via _lp_preview_follow (or invoke preview.sh); refresh-client -S. Window mode: kill-window on the highlighted window, refuse to kill the driver's only window, same shape.
4. OUTPUT: M-BSpace kills the highlighted session and removes it from the list; a neighbour is highlighted + previewed; driver + last session refuse with a message and no kill; deleting the currently-previewed session unlinks the preview first (no orphan leak).
5. DOCS: none beyond P2.M1.T1.S1.

milestones.title: Validation
milestones.description: Integration test suite for rename/delete under the isolated socket.

tasks.title: test_session_mgmt.sh — rename/delete integration suite
tasks.description: Assert: rename opens the prompt pre-filled, a new name updates the tab in place, keeps the highlight, picker stays open; sanitized/collision names abort with a message and no rename; delete kills and removes the highlighted session, a neighbour is highlighted+previewed; driver and last session refuse with a message and no kill; deleting the currently-previewed session unlinks the preview window first (no orphan leak); confirm-delete on prompts y/n.

subtasks.title: Create test_session_mgmt.sh (rename, sanitized/collision, delete, guards, leak, confirm)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: command-prompt is interactive; under the test harness, drive it by sending the new name + Enter via send-keys to the prompt, OR test do-rename directly by invoking session-mgmt.sh do-rename <name> with seeded state (the harness pattern for testing prompt-submit handlers). The leak test mirrors the empirical EXP D setup: link a victim's window into the driver, delete the victim, assert the window is GONE from the driver (list-windows). tests/test_preview.sh has the link/unlink lifecycle idiom with attach_test_client. codebase_patterns.md §P8.
2. INPUT: session-mgmt.sh (P2.M1.T2); the test harness with attach_test_client.
3. LOGIC: Create tests/test_session_mgmt.sh. Tests (isolated -L socket, setup_test pins defer off): (a) rename: seed sessions, activate picker, invoke rename → assert command-prompt opened (or test do-rename directly); submit a valid new name → assert STATE_LIST updated, highlight preserved, picker still active (@livepicker-mode on). (b) sanitized name: do-rename with a name containing ':' or leading '.' → assert rename aborted with a display-message and the session NOT renamed under a different name. (c) collision: do-rename to an existing session name → assert abort + message + no rename. (d) delete: seed ≥3 sessions, activate, delete a non-driver session → assert it is gone (list-sessions), removed from STATE_LIST, a neighbour highlighted+previewed. (e) guard driver: attempt delete on ORIG_SESSION → assert refused + message + no kill. (f) guard last-session: reduce to 1 session, attempt delete → assert refused. (g) leak: preview a victim (link its window into driver), delete the victim → assert the linked window is GONE from the driver (no orphan). (h) confirm-delete on → assert confirm-before prompt (y/n). 
4. OUTPUT: tests/test_session_mgmt.sh passes; covers §15.28 management items.
5. DOCS: none.

backlog.title: Preview sizing: clip instead of reflow (PRD §22)
backlog.description: Independent of P1/P2 (orthogonal — touches the driver's window-size, not the layout or ranking). Adds one option (preview-fit) and one saved-state key (orig-window-size), plus a freeze in activate and a restore in restore.sh. Carries a LOAD-BEARING empirical verification requirement (§22 'Verification required') that gates the implementation.

milestones.title: window-size save/freeze/restore + option (gated by empirical verification)
milestones.description: First confirm clip feasibility on tmux 3.6b (the load-bearing gate), then implement the window-size save (activate) / freeze-to-manual / restore (restore.sh) + the preview-fit option. The empirical verification (T1) decides whether clip is shipped as default or falls back to reflow.

tasks.title: Empirical verification of clip feasibility on tmux 3.6b (load-bearing, gates implementation)
tasks.description: Before coding the freeze, confirm on the isolated socket that (a) a window-size manual session clips an oversized active window (bottom row hidden, not force-resized), (b) set-option -t isolates to the driver, (c) the self-session window present at the status-grow moment does not reflow. Record findings. If the self-window clip is clean, ship clip as default; the linked-candidate link-time resize stays the documented limitation.

subtasks.title: Run + document clip verification on 3.6b; decide clip vs reflow default
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: MOST of this is ALREADY DONE — see plan/003_77ef311abf10/architecture/empirical_findings.md (Finding 1: manual clips an oversized non-shared window, CONFIRMED; Finding 2: shared linked windows reflow ~1 row on status-grow, CONFIRMED partial). This subtask EXTENDS/confirms those in the FULL plugin lifecycle (freeze → grow status → self-window preview → nav re-link) using the test harness's attach_test_client, and records the decision. The key question to settle: does freezing the driver to manual BEFORE the status grow prevent the SELF-session window (the initial preview) from reflowing? empirical EXP A says yes (window stayed 30); EXP F's exact heights were timing-sensitive — confirm the freeze-before-grow ordering yields a non-reflowing self-window.
2. INPUT: The isolated -L socket + attach_test_client pattern; the empirical_findings.md results.
3. LOGIC: Run a focused experiment mirroring the real activate flow: isolated socket, attach client, create a multi-pane driver window, set window-size manual, grow status to 2, assert the self-window's pane layout is UNCHANGED (no reflow) by diffing window_layout before vs after. Then link a candidate window (post-grow) and assert it comes in at the driver size (link-time resize, documented) with no PER-NAV additional reflow. Write the findings + the decision (clip default vs reflow) to plan/003_77ef311abf10/architecture/clip_verification.md. If the self-window reflows despite manual, document that clip is infeasible and the default must be reflow (or snapshot).
4. OUTPUT: clip_verification.md states whether clip is shipped as default and documents the residual (linked-candidate link-time resize). This GATES P3.M1.T2 — do not implement the freeze until this decision is recorded.
5. DOCS: none (internal research doc).

tasks.title: window-size save/freeze/restore + preview-fit option
tasks.description: Add opt_preview_fit (clip). Add ORIG_WINDOW_SIZE to the saved-state contract. In activate, when clip, save the driver's current window-size then set-option -t driver window-size manual BEFORE the status grow. In restore STEP 4, restore the driver's window-size to ORIG_WINDOW_SIZE. reflow mode skips the freeze/restore entirely. Reconcile the §10 step-4 ordering (freeze first, then grow status).

subtasks.title: Add opt_preview_fit + ORIG_WINDOW_SIZE; freeze in activate; restore in restore.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: empirical_findings.md Finding 1 (CONFIRMED) — window-size is PER-SESSION: `set-option -t "$ORIG_SESSION" window-size manual` isolates to the driver; save/restore the driver's value with -t. PRD §22 §3.48 mechanism: save the driver's current window-size, set-option -t driver manual BEFORE the status grow (reorder §10 step 4: freeze first, then grow status). restore.sh STEP 4: shrink status back (already there), then restore window-size to ORIG_WINDOW_SIZE. reflow mode (the legacy fallback if clip is infeasible per P3.M1.T1) skips the freeze/restore entirely. Read scripts/livepicker.sh activate T3 (the status-grow block) and scripts/restore.sh STEP 4 for the exact insertion points.
2. INPUT: opt_preview_fit + ORIG_WINDOW_SIZE (add to options.sh + state.sh); the activate T3 status-grow block; the restore STEP 4 block; the clip decision from clip_verification.md (P3.M1.T1).
3. LOGIC: options.sh — add opt_preview_fit() default "clip" (or "reflow" if P3.M1.T1 decided clip is infeasible). state.sh — add `readonly ORIG_WINDOW_SIZE="@livepicker-orig-window-size"` to the ORIG_* block (auto-cleared). livepicker.sh activate — when opt_preview_fit is clip: save the driver's current window-size `tmux_save_opt`-style into ORIG_WINDOW_SIZE (read `tmux show-option -gv window-size` — the effective global default; document the per-session-override assumption), then `tmux set-option -t "$ORIG_SESSION" window-size manual` BEFORE the status-grow case block. restore.sh STEP 4 — restore `tmux set-option -t "$ORIG_SESSION" window-size "$(get_state "$ORIG_WINDOW_SIZE" "latest")"` (shrink status first is already STEP 4's status restore; window-size restore goes after the status restore so the panes return to natural size). reflow: skip both. Update §9/§10/§7 in-code comments.
4. OUTPUT: clip mode freezes the driver before status grow and restores it on exit; reflow mode is unchanged. The self-session preview does not reflow on status grow (per the P3.M1.T1 verification).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add @livepicker-preview-fit row to README.md Configuration table. Reconcile the bugfix-001 'Detached candidate resize' limitation note: §22 clip addresses the STATUS-GROW reflow (panes shrinking when the extra status line appears); it does NOT eliminate the link-time resize of a detached candidate to the driver's size (that persists, with the snapshot workaround). Distinguish the two in the note. Full prose in P4.

milestones.title: Validation
milestones.description: Integration test for the clip behavior (or the reflow fallback if clip was deemed infeasible).

tasks.title: test_preview_clip.sh — clip/restore integration suite
tasks.description: Assert: on activation (clip mode) the status grows to two lines but the self-session preview's panes do not reflow (diff window_layout before/after); navigating re-links without per-nav reflow; after exit the driver's window-size is restored and panes return to natural size. If P3.M1.T1 showed reflow is forced, assert the reflow fallback path instead and document.

subtasks.title: Create test_preview_clip.sh (clip no-reflow, restore window-size, reflow fallback)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: mirror tests/test_preview.sh's lifecycle (needs attach_test_client). The no-reflow assertion: diff the driver's active window_layout (or pane heights) BEFORE activation vs AFTER status-grow (clip mode) — they must be unchanged. The restore assertion: after exit, `show-options -t driver -v window-size` equals the pre-activation value. empirical_findings.md Finding 2 documents the residual: linked candidates reflow ~1 row at LINK time (post-grow) — assert this is bounded (one-time, not per-nav) and matches the documented limitation, OR if P3.M1.T1 decided reflow default, assert the reflow path. Read clip_verification.md (P3.M1.T1) for the exact expected behavior to assert.
2. INPUT: The clip implementation (P3.M1.T2); attach_test_client; clip_verification.md's recorded decision.
3. LOGIC: Create tests/test_preview_clip.sh. Tests (isolated socket + client): (a) clip mode: record driver active window_layout before activate; activate (status grows to 2); assert window_layout UNCHANGED (self-session no reflow). (b) nav: link a candidate, assert no ADDITIONAL reflow on subsequent nav (link-time resize only). (c) restore: exit picker; assert `show-options -t driver -v window-size` == pre-activation value; assert panes returned to natural size (layout restored). (d) reflow fallback: set @livepicker-preview-fit reflow; activate; assert status grows and the window IS resized (the legacy path) and restores. If clip was deemed infeasible by P3.M1.T1, the (a)/(b) assertions flip to the reflow expectation.
4. OUTPUT: tests/test_preview_clip.sh passes; covers §15.22 + §22 verification.
5. DOCS: none.

backlog.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B)
backlog.description: Depends on P1, P2, P3. Sweeps all four features into the README overview and CHANGELOG. Do NOT edit PRD.md (read-only). This is the catch-all that prevents a coherent delta from shipping with a stale README.

milestones.title: README + CHANGELOG changeset sync
milestones.description: Fold the per-feature Mode-A doc rows/notes added throughout P1-P3 into a coherent README, and add a CHANGELOG entry for the whole changeset.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: README.md: add a Status line subsection (query bar / ranked tabs / scroll / overflow; index/total gone), a Session management note (rename C-r, delete M-BSpace, guards, caveats), update the Appearance note (§19 viewport governs window-status tabs too), reconcile the bugfix-001 limitation with §22 clip. CHANGELOG.md: add an [Unreleased] entry for all four features with defaults + invariants + escape hatches. Follow write-tech-docs style (no em dashes, no marketing tell-words).

subtasks.title: README.md changeset sync (status line, session mgmt, appearance, limitation reconcile)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md already has a Configuration table (per-feature Mode-A rows were added inline by P1.M1.T3.S1, P2.M1.T1.S1, P3.M1.T2.S1). This subtask FOLDS them into coherent prose and adds the cross-cutting overview. Read README.md fully first. Follow the write-tech-docs skill style (load /home/dustin/.pi/agent/skills/write-tech-docs/SKILL.md): no em dashes, no marketing tell-words, no hedging/formulaic transitions. The §19 viewport governs BOTH plain and window-status tabs (PRD §17 diff). Reconcile bugfix-001's 'Detached candidate resize' note with §22 clip: status-grow reflow is now clipped; link-time resize of a detached candidate persists with the snapshot workaround.
2. INPUT: The implemented features (P1 rank/layout/scroll, P2 mgmt, P3 clip); the existing README.md.
3. LOGIC: README.md — (a) add a 'Status line' subsection describing the query bar (icon + query, far-left, only while typing), ranked tabs (left-to-right, fuzzy), scroll viewport + overflow indicators (+N> total hidden, < presence), and that the index/total count is gone. (b) add a 'Session management' note: rename C-r (command-prompt pre-filled), delete M-BSpace (guards: driver/last-session refuse; optional confirm-delete), delete-key terminal caveat (M-BSpace may be stripped on some terminals/SSH/mosh; rebind C-h/DC), escaping limitation (names with quotes/backticks/$ may break the prompt). (c) update Appearance: the §19 viewport governs window-status tabs too (query pinned left, tabs left-to-right, overflow indicators; status-justify only when no query + tabs fit). (d) reconcile the detached-candidate-resize limitation with §22 clip (distinguish status-grow reflow [now clipped] from link-time resize [persists, snapshot workaround]). Do NOT edit PRD.md.
4. OUTPUT: README.md reflects all four features coherently; no stale references to index/total or show-count.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the documentation sync task.

subtasks.title: CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry (4 features, defaults, invariants, escape hatches)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: CHANGELOG.md follows the existing entry format (read it; the bugfix-001 entry is the template). write-tech-docs style (no em dashes, no marketing tell-words). The invariants that must hold (PRD §14): no client-session-changed while browsing; clear_all_state still tears down everything including the new scroll/client-width/window-size keys; exactly one switch-client at confirm. Defaults: preview-fit clip, preview-defer on, tab-style plain, nerd-fonts on, confirm-delete off. Escape hatches: reflow (preview-fit), plain (tab-style), preview-defer off, snapshot (preview-mode).
2. INPUT: The implemented features + their defaults; the existing CHANGELOG.md format.
3. LOGIC: CHANGELOG.md — add an [Unreleased] entry (or the next-version header per the existing format) listing the four features: fuzzy ranking (subsequence + score, replaces substring filter), scrollable status-line layout (query bar + overflow indicators, index/total removed), session management (rename C-r / delete M-BSpace), preview clip (window-size manual freeze, kills status-grow reflow). Note the defaults, that none changes the pollution/restore invariants, and that the reflow/plain/preview-defer-off/snapshot escape hatches remain. Follow the existing format.
4. OUTPUT: CHANGELOG.md has a coherent entry for the changeset.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the documentation sync task.
